fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arkansas Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Arkansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity arkansas, how to transfer dietitian license to arkansas, dietitian compact arkansas, arkansas dietitian endorsement application

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: $110 standard initial application fee (temporarily reduced through June 30, 2026)
quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Annual + 12 CE hours (*Renewal fee temporarily reduced to $3.00 for 2025-2026*)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice dietetics in Arkansas, overseen by the Arkansas Dietetics Licensing Board.

license_types.title: Provisional Licensed Dietitian (PLD)
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have completed all education and supervised practice requirements and are eligible to take the CDR exam but have not yet passed it. Valid for 18 months and allows practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian. Standard fee is $150 (temporarily reduced to $8 for 2025-2026).

steps.title: Register for an Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Arkansas Professional Licensing System (https://www.ark.org/ardlb_app/) and click 'REGISTER' under the 'New Applicant Registration' section to create your account with a username, password, and security questions.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Arkansas Professional Licensing System, select the appropriate application type (e.g., 'Dietitian Initial Application' or 'Licensure by Endorsement'), and fill out all required sections of the application form.

steps.title: Upload Required Documents
steps.description: Electronically attach all necessary supporting documents, such as official transcripts showing a bachelor's or master's degree in dietetics, verification of a supervised practice program (e.g., internship), and verification of passing the CDR examination, as specified in the application.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable initial application fee of $100 through the Arkansas Professional Licensing System using an accepted payment method.

steps.title: Submit Application
steps.description: Review your completed application and all uploaded documents for accuracy and completeness, then formally submit it through the online system.

documents.detail: Application form with your notarized signature.
Copy of your current, active CDR registration card (for full licensure).
Made payable to the Arkansas Dietetics Practice Fund.

provisional_text: Arkansas offers a Provisional Licensed Dietitian (PLD) credential for individuals who have completed their education and internship but have not yet passed the CDR exam. It allows the individual to work under the supervision of an Arkansas Licensed Dietitian. The PLD license is valid for 18 months and can be renewed once. An application for a provisional license requires verification of internship completion and a letter from your prospective employment supervisor.
reciprocity_text: Dietitians applying for licensure in Arkansas who are currently licensed in another state must follow the standard application process and submit a copy of their active CDR registration card, which serves as proof of meeting the national standards recognized by the Arkansas board.

faqs.question: How much does an Arkansas dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The standard initial license fee is $110. However, the Arkansas Dietetics Licensing Board has announced a temporary fee reduction down to $6.00 for the period from July 1, 2025, to June 30, 2026.

faqs.question: When does the Arkansas license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: The Arkansas dietitian license is renewed annually. The standard renewal fee is $50 (temporarily reduced to $3.00 for the 2025-2026 cycle).

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Arkansas requires 12 continuing education units (CEUs) annually to maintain state licensure. For your CDR registration, 75 CEUs are required every five years.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Arkansas. To work in Arkansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arkansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Dietitians applying for licensure in Arkansas who are currently licensed in another state must follow the standard application process and submit a copy of their active CDR registration card, which serves as proof of meeting the national standards recognized by the Arkansas board.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Arkansas
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian license in Arkansas, you must use the Arkansas Professional Licensing System to submit your initial application, including licensure by endorsement.

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account and begin your application, navigate to the Arkansas Professional Licensing System portal, click 'REGISTER' under the 'New Applicant Registration' section, and follow the prompts to set up your username, password, and security questions.
